task_completion.description: Calculate the billing cycle durations for each period.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new column or section in the spreadsheet showing the number of days between bill dates, with values typically ranging from 28 to 32.

task_completion.description: Calculate the daily usage averages for each billing period.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new column showing daily usage, which should be the total usage divided by the billing cycle duration (e.g., values around 25-40 kWh/day).

task_completion.description: Calculate the month-over-month changes in usage or bill amount.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new column showing the difference (absolute or percentage) between the current month's usage/bill and the previous month's.

task_completion.description: Identify high-usage periods using conditional formatting.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Cells in the Usage column (or related columns) have background colors, color scales, or data bars applied, specifically highlighting the high summer values (June, July, August).

task_completion.description: Address data quality issues such as estimated readings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The rows or cells corresponding to March and October (which contain 'Estimated' readings) are visually distinguished, filtered, adjusted, or noted with comments/flags.
The spreadsheet application's title bar shows 'utility_analysis.xlsx', or a file manager window shows the file saved in the Documents folder.

integrity.description: The agent used a GUI spreadsheet application (like LibreOffice Calc) to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the data being manipulated inside a spreadsheet GUI, not just viewed in a text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: Conditional formatting was applied using the application's built-in features.
integrity.visual_evidence: The highlighting of high-usage periods looks like standard conditional formatting (e.g., color scales, uniform cell background fills) rather than manual text highlighting.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI by using a Python or bash script to generate the Excel file directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is evidence of manual interaction with the spreadsheet software (e.g., formulas in the formula bar, active cell selections, open dialog boxes) rather than just terminal commands executing a script.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains 12 months of utility bills from Jan 15 to Dec 14, 2023. Billing cycle durations range from 28 to 32 days. There are exactly two 'Estimated' readings: March and October. High usage periods occur in the summer (June: 1050 kWh, July: 1180 kWh, August: 1220 kWh). Average monthly usage is 943.3 kWh.
